raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The artifact foregrounds an explicit gap statement and contribution claim (downstream-grounded composite metric + eval suite); 'check this' plausibly includes verifying whether the framing correctly reconstructs the gap and whether the stated contribution actually closes it.","R1.2":"The artifact runs a logical chain — WER shortcomings → corpus/pipeline design → quantitative findings → metric calibration → contribution claim; coherence across those steps is a natural target of any 'check'.","R1.3":"Multiple precise empirical claims are present (2.4× underestimation factor, r=0.91 vs 0.62, rank disagreement on 4 of 6 systems); checking whether these are adequately grounded in the described experimental setup is a central responsibility.","R1.6":"'Check this' frequently denotes a proofreading or writing-quality pass; sentence and paragraph quality review is a plausible primary intent and cannot be ruled out."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should 'check this' focus primarily on writing and proofreading quality (R1.6), or on verifying the logical and empirical claims in the text (R1.2, R1.3)?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact foregrounds an explicit gap statement and contribution claim (downstream-grounded composite metric + eval suite); 'check this' plausibly includes verifying whether the framing correctly reconstructs the gap and whether the stated contribution actually closes it.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact runs a logical chain — WER shortcomings → corpus/pipeline design → quantitative findings → metric calibration → contribution claim; coherence across those steps is a natural target of any 'check'.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple precise empirical claims are present (2.4× underestimation factor, r=0.91 vs 0.62, rank disagreement on 4 of 6 systems); checking whether these are adequately grounded in the described experimental setup is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: 'Check this' frequently denotes a proofreading or writing-quality pass; sentence and paragraph quality review is a plausible primary intent and cannot be ruled out.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'check this' focus primarily on writing and proofreading quality (R1.6), or on verifying the logical and empirical claims in the text (R1.2, R1.3)?
